name: SourcedResearch
description: Structured research workflow that produces verifiable, citation-backed answers. Use when the task explicitly requires traceable sources that the user can check — e.g. fact-checking, literature surveys, or any answer where fabricated references are unacceptable. Provides a three-step pipeline: search → extract excerpt → format with citations.

capabilities.name: find_evidence_sources
capabilities.description: STEP 1 of 3 in the sourced-research pipeline. Search the web for sources relevant to a research question and return a numbered list of candidate URLs with title and snippet. You MUST call this tool first before fetching any page content. Only pass URLs from this tool's output to extract_evidence_excerpt — do not invent or reuse URLs from memory.

query.description: The research question or search query.

max_results.description: Maximum number of candidate sources to return (default: 6).

capabilities.name: extract_evidence_excerpt
capabilities.description: STEP 2 of 3 in the sourced-research pipeline. Fetch a URL returned by find_evidence_sources and extract a focused excerpt (up to max_chars characters of body text). Call this once per URL you intend to cite. The returned excerpt — not your memory — MUST be the basis for every claim in the final answer. Do not call this tool on URLs not provided by find_evidence_sources.

url.description: URL to fetch. Must be a URL returned by find_evidence_sources.

max_chars.description: Maximum body text characters to return (default: 6000).

capabilities.name: format_answer_with_citations
capabilities.description: STEP 3 of 3 in the sourced-research pipeline. Compose the final answer and attach the supporting citations. Each citation entry MUST contain a non-empty url and a non-empty excerpt drawn directly from extract_evidence_excerpt output — not paraphrased or invented. Call this tool only after you have called extract_evidence_excerpt for every URL you plan to cite. The tool validates that citations are non-empty and returns a formatted Markdown answer with a Sources section. IMPORTANT: pass 'citations' as a native JSON array of objects, NOT as a serialized string. Correct: citations=[{"url":"...","excerpt":"..."}]. Wrong: citations="[{\"url\":\"...\"}]".

answer.description: The answer text to present to the user. Every factual claim should be supported by at least one citation in the citations list.

citations.description: List of citation objects, one per source used. Pass as a native array — do NOT stringify it.

excerpt.description: Verbatim or near-verbatim excerpt from the source that supports the claim (must be non-empty).
